items.title: Harden strategy-guide injection surface (MED-1 newline framing, MED-2 read-time re-validate, LOW-3 cap)
items.landed_on: integration/next (cycle 72, 2026-07-10) — SHIPPED to main via bundle PR #130, merge d59265993504ff542838bb20dccfefd91de9431e. TestFlight v1.0.1162 (build 202607101611).
items.resolution: All 3 fixes + doc correction landed on integration/next @ fe2570f, backend-only. MED-1: format_guide_line flattens per-fragment whitespace (' '.join(frag.split())) so internal \n/\t can't survive; validate_guide also rejects any \n/\r-bearing field (defense-in-depth) — kills the multi-line 'section header' mimicry into BOTH mouths. MED-2: /course-intel (caddie.py:1261) re-validates the persisted guide at READ time against intel.hazards, at loop-body level before the session cache write — BOTH the text mouth (caddie.py:728) and realtime/voice mouth (voice_prompts.py:159) read session.hole_intel populated from that loop, so it covers both; reviewer confirmed NO bypass site (/recommend doesn't call format_guide_line). Ungrounded guide dropped to None (no line, no crash). LOW-3: _MAX_FIELD_CHARS applied per common_mistakes item. LOW-1/2: plan §9/§10 corrected — injection_pattern keyword scan is defense-in-depth not a boundary; no classifier added. Each fix has a RED-before test (verified: 7 target tests fail on pre-fix source, 186 offline guide/caddie tests GREEN with fix). CI on head d315570: Backend gate SUCCESS (ruff + pytest incl route/integration on Postgres) + Frontend gate SUCCESS. Reviewer: SHIP (adversarial security lens). Follow-up carded: caddie-guide-session-reload-revalidate.
items.why: Security review 2026-07-10 (cycle 71) of the shipped web-content-into-LLM strategy-guide surface — core mitigations CONFIRMED real (no-HTML/server-search, structured parse, grounding validation with teeth, failure-honesty, owner-auth), no HIGH. But 2 MED + 1 cheap LOW to fix: MED-1 — format_guide_line (guide_writer.py:70-84) only .strip()s, internal \n survives + validate_guide doesn't reject it → a multi-line field mimics a new prompt-section header injected into BOTH caddie mouths (confirmed exploitable). Fix: normalize whitespace per fragment (' '.join(frag.split())) AND reject any field containing \n in validate_guide. MED-2 — validate_guide runs only at WRITE time (course_guides.py:121); read path (course_intel.py:208-216) injects the stored blob verbatim → guides written by an older/weaker validator bypass current controls FOREVER. Fix: re-validate at read time — hazards available at caddie.py:1236, add `if intel.strategy_guide: intel.strategy_guide = validate_guide(intel.strategy_guide, intel.hazards)`. LOW-3 — apply _MAX_FIELD_CHARS per common_mistakes item. LOW-1/2 note-only: don't overstate injection_pattern in plan §9 (trivially bypassable, defense-in-depth only). Each fix needs a test that goes RED pre-fix.

items.title: Re-validate strategy guides on caddie session RELOAD too (stale session-blob edge)
items.why: Non-blocking follow-up flagged by the cycle-72 security reviewer. MED-2 re-validates persisted guides at /course-intel read time (which repopulates session.hole_intel), but a caddie session persisted by PRE-FIX code holds its guide in session.hole_intel JSONB and is NOT re-run through validate_guide on session RELOAD (session.py:~77). This is a stale-data edge, NOT a new injection surface: the durable 'cached forever' store is the course JSONB (re-validated on every /course-intel call), and the MED-1 renderer flatten neutralizes the actual multi-line framing vector regardless. Fix: run validate_guide (against the reloaded hazards) when hydrating strategy_guide from a persisted session blob, so grounding re-checks also apply on session load. Small + low-priority; add a RED-before test on a session hydrated with a now-invalid guide.
items.resolution: shipped-to-main (00d7508, PR #132). SILENT — security defense-in-depth. Re-validate a persisted strategy_guide on caddie SESSION reload: validate_guide(intel.strategy_guide, intel.hazards) at the _row_to_session hydrate seam (session.py), grounded against the hazards persisted in the SAME HoleIntelligence blob — same fail-closed contract as the MED-2 /course-intel read-time fix (caddie.py:1261-1262). Covers BOTH caddie mouths (text + realtime read session.hole_intel, sole hydrate path). Closes the last thread of the 2026-07-10 guide-hardening review. Stale-data edge only (durable course JSONB already re-validated per /course-intel; renderer-flatten neutralizes framing vector). RED-before test backend/tests/test_session_guide_revalidate.py (drop-test fails on pre-fix code; guards: valid survives ==, absent no-op). Reviewer SHIP (6 points verified, no blocking). QA: ruff clean, 3/3 new + 99 guide-suite pass, 2050 collected clean, frontend untouched (lint/tsc/voice-274 green). Owner replied 'Ship it' on bundle PR #132; merged to main.

items.title: Caddie advice-quality eval harness (golden set + LLM-judge, CI gate)
items.why: AUDIT 2026-07-08 (caddie-excellence-audit P1.5; area G = grade D). No advice-QUALITY harness exists — caddie tests cover geometry/plumbing only, runtime LLM output has no automated quality gate, so 'good enough to replace a caddie' is unfalsifiable and every other caddie improvement is unmeasurable. Build a git-diffable golden JSONL (30-50 scenarios: hole geometry + player + question -> expected advice properties, incl. edge cases: dogleg, hazard carry, downhill lie, low-confidence parse). Two tiers per SOTA: deterministic assertions first (names a real club? sane yardage bounds? no hallucinated hazard? valid shape) + an LLM-JUDGE rubric ('grounded in the actual hole? accounts for stated lie/wind? appropriately concise?'); regression-gate in CI; de-bias the judge (judge with a DIFFERENT model than the one under test, randomize order, control for verbosity). Extends the existing offline voice-tests gate pattern. Silent. UNBLOCKED.
items.resolution: done-on-bundle (PR #117, silent; f491b71 on integration/next; reviewer SOUND — harness honest/falsifiable, 3 non-blocking notes folded in; Tier-1 47 passed offline + full suite green + ruff clean). Two-tier caddie advice-quality eval: Tier-1 deterministic prompt-assembly+honesty asserts (always in CI, no key/DB) over a 25-scenario golden JSONL seeded from the real hole-4 incidents (no-left-bunker hallucination, observed-reality gaslight, Bethpage-4 side-flip) with a closed check-name registry + teeth tests proving every check goes RED on the pre-fix world; Tier-2 LLM-judge (judge!=candidate, injection-safe, budget-capped, projection-abort) is on-demand/nightly only, triple-gated off CI. Follow-ups: grow golden set 25->30-50 (incident-driven growth rule in README); optional nightly Tier-2 schedule workflow; optional extract _build_session_voice_prompt pure body so the text mouth is Tier-2-drivable offline.

items.title: Voice parser: fix 'for'->4 in regex + 'dbl bogey' everyone-pattern (found by unit tests)

items.title: Fix integration-test event-loop teardown (asyncpg attached to a different loop)
items.rationale: tests/integration fail with RuntimeError: ...attached to a different loop / Event loop is closed when run as part of the full pytest suite. Cause: app.db.engine module-level async engine/async_session is bound to one event loop, but pytest-asyncio (asyncio_mode=auto, function-scoped loop) creates a new loop per test. Fix: create/dispose the engine per test (or per session-scoped loop), e.g. recreate engine in a fixture bound to the running loop, or set a session-scoped event_loop, or dispose engine between tests. Then re-promote integration tests to required (promote-integration-tests).
items.done_note: Session-scoped pytest-asyncio loop fixed the "attached to a different loop" bug. Verified: 13 integration tests pass against Postgres in CI (PR #53).

items.title: Clerk session persistence in iOS webview (standardBrowser:false)
items.rationale: Sign-in completed but session did not persist (isSignedIn stayed false -> bounced to signed-out) — iOS WKWebView blocks Clerk third-party session cookie. Fix: standardBrowser:false on ClerkProvider (Clerk official native option, avoids cookies), gated to Capacitor.isNativePlatform() so web/dev unaffected; + CapacitorCookies enabled. WKAppBoundDomains layer REMOVED (risky for Capacitor localhost). Awaiting on-device test.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: shipped v1.0.339 (build 202606280108) with standardBrowser:false + CapacitorCookies (WKAppBoundDomains dropped as too risky for Capacitor). Awaiting owner: sign in -> does session persist (reach home + stay)? | Bundled into COMPLETE native-auth build v1.0.343 (build 202606280228), uploaded; CapacitorPreferences SPM resolved clean. Awaiting owner: sign in -> stays -> force-quit -> still signed in.
items.note: ROOT CAUSE (matches evidence exactly): WebView CORS blocks reading the Authorization response header cross-origin (capacitor://localhost -> clerk.looperapp.org), so the session JWT is never captured (tok never flips). FIX: CapacitorHttp:{enabled:true} routes fetch through native NSURLSession which bypasses browser CORS + reads all headers. + richer copyable diagnostic (auth-hdr field = key signal). Building. WATCH: CapacitorHttp patches global fetch — verify the audio-blob transcribe upload + other API calls still work on-device. | Shipped v1.0.361 (build 202606280650). Awaiting owner: sign in -> auth-hdr/signed true? Copyable diagnostic added. | v1.0.361 diagnostic: native-sent:FALSE (window hooks never fire with CDN clerk-js) — CONFIRMED. Fix: bundle @clerk/clerk-js locally + register FAPI callbacks on the INSTANCE + pass Clerk={instance} to ClerkProvider (the @clerk/expo pattern). Building. | Shipped v1.0.365 (build 202606280936): local @clerk/clerk-js instance + instance callbacks. Awaiting owner: native-sent should now be true. | CRASH ROOT CAUSE: bundled Clerk instance had no UI components -> <SignIn> mountSignIn assertComponentsReady throws -> white-screen. FIXED (d78de56): standard CDN ClerkProvider + guarded window hooks; app loads (sim-verified). NATIVE-AUTH ROOT CAUSE: clerk-react@5/clerk-js@5 dont honor the token hooks -> native-sent never fires. REAL FIX: upgrade clerk-react->v6 (clerk-js@6 honors hooks + ships UI). Building v6 upgrade. Autonomous testing now exists: frontend/ios/SIMTEST.md + simtest-headless.mjs (CI-able crash check). | VERIFIED 2026-06-28 via REAL credentialed sign-in in iPhone-17 sim (WebKit remote inspector + ios-webkit-debug-proxy): @clerk/react 6.11.1 / clerk-js 6.22.0. ALL native-auth signals GREEN: native-sent=true on EVERY FAPI request incl. sign_ins POST/attempt_first_factor (v6 honors window hooks); auth-hdr=true + tok=true (CapacitorHttp made authorization response header readable, JWT captured + persisted to Preferences); napi=true. Password (first factor) ACCEPTED. signed=false reached ONLY because Clerk gated the new device behind an emailed second-factor OTP ('signing in from a new device') which the agent can't read (no inbox) — Clerk product security, orthogonal to native-auth plumbing. CONCLUSION: native-auth fix CONFIRMED working; remaining step is the human entering the email OTP on first sign-in. Shipped verified build v1.0.369 (build 202606281037).

items.title: Harden Realtime voice setup: noise reduction + better transcription + semantic VAD
items.rationale: The preload-removal hotfix (cd2e516) stopped background hallucination, but the root noise/silence-hallucination risk remains because the mint uses whisper-1, no noise reduction, and raw server_vad. In realtime_relay.py mint config: (1) add input_audio_noise_reduction {type:'near_field'} (phone-near-mouth); (2) switch audio.input.transcription.model whisper-1 -> gpt-4o-transcribe (or -mini) — env-configurable (OPENAI_TRANSCRIBE_MODEL) with whisper-1 fallback, since whisper hallucinates on silence; (3) consider turn_detection semantic_vad (eagerness) vs server_vad to cut noise-triggered false turns. items.title: Fix voice-setup chat ordering — user message shows AFTER the caddie's reply
items.rationale: OWNER HIGH PRIORITY (2026-06-28): owner says 'hello', caddie replies, but the caddie's reply renders ABOVE the 'hello'. Cause: the user transcript event (conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.completed) arrives AFTER the assistant's streamed response deltas, and messages render in arrival/insertion order (VoiceRoundSetupRealtime upsert appends new ids in arrival order). Fix: order messages by conversation-item sequence, not arrival — the user's input item is created BEFORE the response item. In realtime.ts handleEvent, capture an ordering key (item creation order / a monotonic seq, or use the item_id ordering the API provides) and sort the rendered list by it; keep streamed assistant deltas coalescing. Add a unit test for the ordering helper.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: built 179d03c on integration/next. New pure MessageOrderTracker + sortByOrder (lib/voice/realtime-ordering.ts): a stable monotonic order key assigned when each conversation ITEM begins (user slot reserved at input_audio_buffer.speech_started, identity-matched by item_id to the transcript; assistant slot at response.created/first delta), NOT when transcript text arrives. realtime.ts: RealtimeMessage gains required `order`, handleEvent threads item_id, sendText emits the typed line centrally + renders even if DC closed. Both renderers sort by order (VoiceRoundSetupRealtime + useRealtimeCaddie). Reviewer adversarial pass: found + fixed a real desync (FIFO user-slot matching corrupted ordering for the rest of the session on phantom/empty/VAD-bounced speech_started -> re-keyed by item_id; added the regression test). 9 unit tests. Gates: lint 0/0 / tsc 0 / voice-tests 265/265 / vitest 374/374 / build clean. DEVICE-ONLY verify: WebRTC live voice ordering must be confirmed on the next TestFlight build. items.title: Fix sheet grabber-handle drag — swiping it scrolls the background page instead of dismissing
items.rationale: OWNER (2026-06-28): swiping DOWN on a sheet's grab handle moves the background page instead of dismissing the sheet. The drag isn't being captured at the handle, so the gesture falls through to page scroll. Audit the drag-to-dismiss sheets (CaddieSheet + the new VoiceRoundSetupRealtime grab handle): ensure the handle's onPointerDown calls dragControls.start(e), the draggable motion.div has the dragControls + dragListener=false wired, and the background/body can't scroll under the sheet (lock body scroll / overscroll-behavior: contain / touch-action on the backdrop) while a sheet is open. Verify on device.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: built 52a1d9a on integration/next. Audit found BOTH sheets already had the handle wired correctly (onPointerDown -> dragControls.start, touch-action:none, dragListener=false, drag y + dragControls on the motion.div). Root cause of the fall-through was the MISSING piece: nothing locked the page behind an open sheet, so any gesture framer-motion didn't claim scrolled the background (esp. iOS WKWebView document rubber-band, which touch-action alone doesn't suppress). Fix: new src/lib/sheet.ts with useBodyScrollLock(locked) (pins <body> position:fixed + overflow hidden + overscroll-behavior none, restores scroll on close) and the pure shouldDismissSheetDrag(offsetY, velocityY) threshold (>120px OR >600px/s) extracted from both sheets. Wired into CaddieSheet (locked on `open`) and VoiceRoundSetupRealtime (locked for mounted lifetime); added touch-action:none + overscroll-behavior:contain to both backdrops. Inner conversation list stays scrollable. 8 unit tests (src/lib/sheet.test.ts: threshold edges + lock apply/restore via jsdom). Gates: lint 0 / tsc 0 / voice-tests 265/265 / vitest 382/382 / build clean. DEVICE-ONLY verify: the drag gesture is pointer/touch-only — confirm on next TestFlight that the handle dismisses and the page no longer scrolls behind an open sheet. NOTICEABLE -> rides the next approval bundle.

items.progress: 2026-06-28: built 727c7df on integration/next. ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis 1, evidence-backed): the Realtime remote audio sink in realtime.ts was document.createElement('audio')+autoplay but NEVER appended to the DOM and had no inline-playback attr. Authoritative iOS guidance (webrtcHacks Safari guide, Capacitor #8176): iOS WKWebView renders remote WebRTC audio through a SINGLE attached element and 'doesn't support simultaneous playing of multiple elements'; a detached autoplay element can leave the track to ALSO render via the audio session -> two slightly-offset copies = 'two overlapping voices'. FIX: make the element the SOLE controlled sink -> append to document.body (hidden), setAttribute('playsinline',''), autoplay, idempotent srcObject assign (only on a different stream), and audioEl.remove() in cleanup() so reconnects/warm-preloads never stack a second sink. start() is guarded (if(this.pc)return) and its error path calls cleanup() -> at most one element per client (verified: hook + VoiceRoundSetupRealtime each hold one clientRef, recreate only after stop()). DEFENSIVE rule-out (hypothesis 2): NO double-response path -> response.create fires only on typed input (sendText) and after a tool result (runTool); minted session uses server_vad with no create_response:false, so voice turns auto-respond exactly once. Mint config untouched (owner: deployed+working). Reviewer (fresh opus context): SOUND/ship, no blocking issues; confirmed no double-sink, SSR-safe, defensive claim holds. Non-blocking nit (pre-existing, NOT the cause, left out of scope): onconnectionstatechange doesn't call cleanup() on a silent network drop. Gates: lint 0 / tsc 0 / voice-tests 265/265 / vitest 399/399 / build clean. DEVICE-ONLY verifiable (audio) -> MUST confirm on next TestFlight build the caddie no longer plays two overlapping voices. NOTICEABLE -> rides the next approval bundle (PR #66).
items.title: Caddie voice plays TWICE (two overlapping voices) on every response
items.rationale: OWNER 2026-06-28 (voice is otherwise much better now): when the caddie responds, two overlapping voices play. Setup uses ONE RealtimeCaddieClient, so NOT two sessions. Top suspects, in order: (1) the WebRTC remote audio element in realtime.ts is created with document.createElement("audio")+autoplay but NEVER appended to the DOM and has no playsInline — on iOS WKWebView the remote track can auto-play via the audio session AND the element → two offset copies. FIX candidate: append the audioEl to document.body (display:none), set playsInline + autoplay, and ensure it is the SOLE sink; remove on cleanup. (2) double response per turn — verify only ONE response.created fires per user turn (no stray response.create; confirm server_vad create_response not combined with an explicit create; confirm interrupt_response cancels an in-progress response so a new turn does not overlap an old one). (3) stale session/audioEl not torn down on reconnect. Device-verify with the owner. HIGH priority.

items.title: Fix caddie TTS playback on iOS (NotSupportedError) + hands-free re-arm
items.why: Prod telemetry from owner's live session: voicetel surface=sheet-tts event=speak_failed detail=NotSupportedError. Caddie spoken replies don't play on his iPhone (Capacitor WKWebView). Also silently breaks the hands-free conversational loop, which re-arms listening only on the audio element's `ended` event. Leading cause: CapacitorHttp-patched fetch corrupts/mistypes the binary mp3 blob; secondary: gesture-unlock bless-plays an element with no src. Fix with persistent-primed-audio + correct-typed blob, add unlock-failure telemetry + deterministic tests.
items.resolution: done-shipped-integration-next (35c4103; bundle #108; SHIP verdict; awaiting owner ship-it)

items.title: Voice telemetry events dropped on iOS (flush on suspend)
items.why: Observed 2026-07-08: voicetel volume is near-blind (~1 event in 4h of a live session). lib/voice/telemetry.ts flushes on an 8s timer / 12-event batch / visibilitychange->hidden with keepalive fetch. On iOS WKWebView (Capacitor), pagehide is more reliable than visibilitychange, and the CapacitorHttp-patched fetch may not honor keepalive when the webview suspends -> queued events die on background/kill before the timer fires. Add a pagehide/freeze listener + verify keepalive actually ships (or use sendBeacon / a smaller batch / immediate flush for rare high-signal events). Don't fix while we're mid-TTS fix; targeted follow-up.
items.resolution: done-shipped-main (rider, shipped via early bundle to main) || (prior resolution) Cycle 19 (1c65b49, rides PR #109). Option A — smallest secure design, no new/unauth surface: added a `flush?:boolean` flag to voiceEvent() that immediately drains the WHOLE queue, wired it on all failure events (mic_error/live_start_failed/live_unsupported/resolved_fallback/speak_failed/prime_failed) so they ship while foregrounded; added a window pagehide listener alongside visibilitychange. Authenticated fetch(keepalive) + Clerk Bearer preserved; sendBeacon/token-in-body rejected (attack surface). Reviewer CLEAN + security-reviewed (auth preserved, no PII, flag never in payload); 10 deterministic jsdom tests. Unblocks device-side latency verification.

items.title: TOP-PRIORITY owner bug: voice caddie speaks incoherent tee-shot yardages (466-300!=125, confabulates backwards physics when challenged) + fabricates miss-side/hazards + always says driver — ground the tee-shot brain
items.resolution: LANDED on integration/next (bundle PR #139) cycle 114 (2026-07-12), NOTICEABLE, awaiting owner ship. Fable plan specs/caddie-numbers-coherence-plan.md. Commits 4a6459c (impl) + 3b7c830 (review-found frame-align fix). ROOT CAUSE of the 125: the realtime orb get_recommendation (POST /caddie/session/recommend) had a hardcoded yards=400 default and never consulted intel.yards on that HTTP path (the no-fake-400 fix had only landed in the text tool loop) -> a 466y hole solved as 400 -> plays-like ~425 -> leave EXACTLY 125. The 300 was the raw bag line; 280/266 were honest physics under the day wind — three truthful-in-isolation numbers from three frames with no shared payload and no closing rule. FIXES: (1) recommend_payload/SessionRecommendRequest.yards Optional, resolves distance_yards or yards or intel.yards else honest error; client forwards resolved yardage/basis via new setToolContext seam. (2) one-solve TeeShotNumbers on CaddieRecommendation, drive from the same shot_distance_for_club get_shot_distance uses (parity by construction), leave_exact = to_green - drive_total closes exactly, one format_tee_numbers_line feeds both mouths. (3) NUMBERS_COHERENCE_RULE + MISS_SIDE_GROUNDING_RULE wired into build_realtime_instructions + both text stable_text blocks + eval registry (verbatim-only, leave = hole - drive, challenge -> re-derive-and-admit never invent physics; never assert a miss-side the hazard payload doesnt support). (4) miss-side tie/both-sides -> center 'no good miss, commit to the fairway' (kills the <= tie-break favor-left). (5) corridor v1 bend-cap: a mapped corner with tree evidence the drive would fly through caps the club to land short (driver-by-default dies for mapped corners). (6) par-sanity guard (par3>280y flags two-shot hole, par suspect). (7) frame-align (reviewer-found): a drive whose physics total reaches the raw green is reachable/approach + signed leave_exact so the printed equation always closes. Trees ARE in the drive-zone evidence today (OSM woods -> Hazard type=trees via drive_zone_hazards, verified); the 'bunker right' was the model relocating greenside bunkers, now impossible under the rule. The hole strategy guide IS consumed in both mouths when cached (regression-locked). reviewer(fable) SHIP (7/8 sound first pass; found+fixed downhill-short frame-mismatch, re-verified non-closing class structurally eliminated, physics parity byte-exact, no reachability regression, tests additive). qa PASS (ruff; backend pytest 2316/95-skip; frontend lint/tsc; vitest 2416; build 19-page; voice 278). 194+ new backend tests. CI backend gate SUCCESS on head 661a28b; frontend gate in progress at land time.
items.why: Owner reported directly with 2 sets of screenshots (Bethpage Black 1 466y par4; Bethpage Red 3): the caddie spoke yardages that dont add up and, when the owner challenged, confabulated backwards physics instead of re-deriving; asserted 'left is the better miss' on a hole tree-lined both sides; and defaulted to driver through a mapped dogleg corner into trees while naming a fabricated bunker. Owner: 'Caddie still needs a lot of work ... it should always think like a true caddie.' Correctness + honesty are the crux of the voice-first product.

(C) DONE 2026-07-12 (owner approved verbatim 'Run the ingest and generate red guides'): Bethpage Red guides generated on the prod box via the sanctioned env-gated run_guide_backfill (GUIDE_BACKFILL_COURSES=269e1f2e…, MAX_COURSES=1, model claude-sonnet-5, one bounded pass, ~11 min). RESULT: 15/18 guides cached; holes 1, 8, 18 validator-REJECTED and negative-cached (honest-empty, same big-bunker carry-centroid strictness as Black 7 & 11 — see guide-validator investigation item; no retry storm by design). Spot-check hole 2 guide is grounded ('missing left brings the fairway bunker at 360 into play' — matches the ingested bunker). Guides are consumed automatically by both caddie mouths when cached (cycle 114 regression-locked). (A) DONE cycle 115 (2026-07-12, integration/next PR #139, NOTICEABLE) — full corridor-WIDTH club selection per specs/corridor-width-club-selection-plan.md (fable plan). DANGER-edge fit rule (tree/water lines, NOT fairway edges — the fable plan FALSIFIED the fairway-edge premise: a ±1.5σ dispersion window of 56y for a 15-hcp driver vs a 30-40y fairway would cap every player to an 8-iron on every tee; fairway cross-sections stored as color only). New HoleIntelligence.corridor (additive/defaulted) computed in routes/caddie.py /course-intel loop via hazards.extract_corridor_profile (perpendicular cross-sections every 10y along the golf=hole centerline; danger edges from _tree_observations/_water_observations with min-obs 3/1; all-or-nothing None gate); consumed in aim_point.py positioning branch AFTER v1 bend-cap with take-the-shorter ceiling; unknown-width never rejects. WHY note grounded in payload numbers only. Honesty finding: Bethpage fixture has 99 fairway polys/90 holes but ZERO woods/tree features -> profile None -> v1 bend-cap byte-identical where danger data absent (no pseudo-precision; activates automatically as ingest improves). [CORRECTED cycle 117, 2026-07-13 — the 'ZERO woods/tree' claim was a STALE TEST-FIXTURE artifact, NOT prod/live reality. Verified: (i) the OSM ingest ALREADY fetches+assigns+stores woods/trees end-to-end since a5bef42 (2026-06-29, predating both prod ingests); (ii) live Overpass at Bethpage has 1527 tree nodes + 162 woods ways (base ts 2026-06-12, unchanged); (iii) PROD DB rows ALREADY contain it — Black tree=1038/woods=5 (all 18 holes), Red tree=59/woods=8 (holes 1,4,5,6,13,14,15,16,17,18) — byte-matching a fresh fetch; (iv) end-to-end on prod data w/ deployed engine: Black 13 (134 trees) yields corridor=16/31 width-samples 68-78y AND 4 tree-hazards (2L/2R) — corridor-width club selection is LIVE; Black 1 / Red 1 / Red 18 flow tree-hazards into miss-side grounding (corridor gate not met -> honest bend-cap). So the corridor/tree brain is ALREADY LIVE on Bethpage as of v1.1.6 (consumption deployed + data present) — no code change, no re-ingest (would be a no-op on identical data; idempotency already reviewer-verified). GENUINE remaining gap: Red 3 (and other sparse-tree holes) have zero OSM tree data -> stay bend-cap; corridor-width needs dense two-sided tree mapping (Black well-mapped, Red sparse). Denser tree coverage = a data-source question (OSM tree density / satellite-CV spike #123), a product decision, NOT a bug — filed as bethpage-red-tree-coverage-gap.] Commits 8703b4d (impl) + 485c664 (reviewer-found coherence guard: width club swap requires a genuine rejected_club, prevents a stale bend-cap note on a sub-yard rounding tie). reviewer(fable) SHIP (geometry hand-re-derived in a rotated frame — side asymmetry, dogleg local-heading, tee anchoring all confirmed; v1 byte-intact) · qa PASS (ruff; backend 2337 non-DB; tsc/lint; voice 278/278; vitest 2417). items.title: TOP-PRIORITY owner request: yardage-book tee-shot overlays on the in-round satellite hole map (blue/white/red 200/150/100 plates from green center + fairway-bunker front/back carries), tee-shot context only
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-12 (bundle #137 merged @c715d2e; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.5 build 202607121234). Previously: LANDED on integration/next (bundle PR #137) @8de8c0d (cycle 113, 2026-07-12), NOTICEABLE (on-course satellite-map feature). Fable plan specs/tee-shot-yardage-overlays-plan.md. Frontend-only pure module frontend/src/lib/map/tee-shot-overlays.ts (+ tee-shot-overlays.test.ts, 25 pinned vitest) consumed by GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx via a new optional mappedHoles prop (inert when absent -> CourseSearch/CourseScoutMap//map/course unaffected); wired through InlineHoleDiagram (holeIndex) + RoundPageClient (mappedHolesIndex). Data reality: the honest geometry (real bunker Polygons + the dogleg-aware golf=hole centerline LineString) lives in CourseData.holes[i].features (already client-side for the paper HoleDiagram); the map's CourseCoordinates.hazards are centroids only, so this reads features directly. PLATES: walk the golf=hole centerline green-first (60y green-end sanity guard) in UNROUNDED float meters, green-CENTER offset so a '150' is truly 150 to center, omit targets beyond path length; centerline REQUIRED (no chord fallback -> dogleg honesty); mechanical honesty guard greenEndLateralOffsetMeters omits ALL plates when the way's green-end is >7y perpendicular off the line to green center (catches offset-way mislabel; leaves legitimate downpath doglegs intact). BUNKERS: Polygon outer-ring vertices only, carry projected in a tee-origin equirectangular frame (matches backend hazards._xy_m / _project_onto_polyline), front=min/back=max round-to-5, fairway predicate on RAW carry (in [100,330]) AND >=45y radial from green center (excludes greenside) AND min|lateral|<=45y (corridor); centroid-only bunkers SKIPPED (never a fabricated range); display cap 2 inline / 4 fullscreen (smallest-lateral selection). VISIBILITY: pure stateless predicate (position null OR !gpsOnHole OR dist(pos,tee)<=40y), native overlays touched only on the boolean FLIP (no GPS-jitter churn). Par-3 fully suppressed. RENDER (iOS iconUrl can't do data-URL icons — verified Map.swift): plates = native addCircles (4m; colors reuse the existing tee palette #2e5aa8/#f2efe6/#b23a2e), bunker near-edge dot = native circle (3m), carry TEXT = DOM paper chips styled like the tap-target pill (right edge, AnimatePresence). All native calls gated on mapReadyRef; overlays id-tracked in a separate teeShotCircleIdsRef + routed through the coalescing camera queue (no leak across hole swipes). reviewer (FABLE, trust-critical geometry) SHIP: hand-verified green-center offset, dogleg plate on 2nd leg, carry orientation (a real TDD-caught inversion fix — tee-first reversal + teeAlongM subtract), unrounded walk, honesty (synthetic hd.front/back never read, degenerate->EMPTY), onMapReady gating, no regression, security nil; 4 non-blocking, #1(plate self-check)/#3(raw-carry predicate)/#4(test6 deadband) folded in, #2(pre-existing mount/queue interleave) out of scope. qa PASS + CI Frontend+Backend SUCCESS on head 3ca8324 (lint/tsc/vitest 2416/build 19-page/voice 278). designer BLOCKING->APPROVE on rendered pixels: 1st pass BLOCKED (inline chip stack overlapped RoundPageClient's Par/Yardage/Hcp stats pill; cap-4 too dense inline) -> fixed (chip top 12->54 = 11px gap measured at 390x844+375x667; inline cap 2) -> APPROVE (both blockers resolved, no regression). OWNER REAL-DEVICE EYEBALL NEEDED (not a gate fail): on-satellite plate/dot contrast + size across live pinch-zoom (sandbox has no live Maps tiles — Maps-key credential policy), and the AnimatePresence fade as GPS crosses the 40y tee-zone boundary on a real walk.
items.why: Owner-requested directly with a reference PDF of a real Jones yardage book: 'add some details like this for TEE SHOTS ONLY... the blue/white/red indicators on the map to show 200,150,100 yards out... showing the yardages to the front and back of the bunkers (if there are any fairway bunkers) from the tee box are super helpful... only when we are on the tee box / open a new hole... makes it look more like a true yardage book.' Explicitly rejected the per-dot fairway-yardage scatter as 'too much.' This is the crux yardage-book surface — trust-critical (the numbers a golfer reads MUST be right) and honest (no geometry -> silent absence, never invented).

items.title: Owner bug (v1.1.5): Bethpage Red hole 1 shows no tee-shot overlays and no hole centerline — backfill Red course geometry into the prod DB
items.resolution: AUDIT DONE cycle 115 (2026-07-12); FIX is an owner-gated prod write. Method: OSM-source audit (local Overpass, no DB/secrets) via new backend/scripts/audit_course_coverage.py (landed on integration/next @9c95dad, bundle PR #139, SILENT dev tooling; generalises the Black-only diag_bethpage.py to any --target and adds centerline-presence + overlay-ready verdict per hole). Finding: OSM has FULL Bethpage Red geometry — 18/18 holes overlay-ready (Red H1 466y par4: centerline + green + 2 fairway + 3 tees; 0 bunkers is an honest OSM gap). So upstream OSM is NOT the gap; root cause (high confidence) is Red not (fully) ingested into the prod DB (only Black is; ingest defaults to Black). Verified fix end-to-end via dry-run (no prod write): uv run backend/scripts/ingest_osm_course.py --target-course Red --course-key osm-bethpage-red --course-name 'Bethpage Red' — assembles 18 holes / 231 features under a NEW osm-bethpage-red course UUID 269e1f2e-65cc-5cf6-a9b0-f5908e298155, SEPARATE from Black (cannot corrupt Black; reviewer verified all 5 data-safety claims TRUE @9c95dad). GATED: prod DB write — owner (or an approved session) runs it against prod, then verifies with audit_course_coverage.py --target Red. SECONDARY: the owner's EXISTING Red round only gains overlays if its mappedCourseId points at the new Red row; new rounds via course search will. INFRA BLOCKER for the DB-side audit: the looper/prod Secrets Manager DATABASE_URL has a stale password (blocked the sanctioned SSM read-probe); refresh it to re-enable DB coverage checks. EXECUTED 2026-07-12 (owner approved verbatim 'Run the ingest and generate red guides'): ingest run ON the prod box via SSM (scripts materialized from origin/integration/next @1658715 into /tmp, run with the app's own .env in-process — Secrets Manager DISABLED so the stale DATABASE_URL was never consulted; deployed checkout branch untouched on main). Result: 18 holes / 231 features / 18 elevations written under Red UUID 269e1f2e (one transient Overpass 504, auto-retried). DB-verified via in-process get_course: Red 18/18 overlay-ready (H1 = centerline + green + 2 fairway + 3 tees, 0 bunkers = honest OSM gap); Black 2b8caab5 UNCHANGED (18/18 overlay-ready, 1248 features, guides intact 16/18 — 7 & 11 pre-existing validator honest-empties). Local search leg (list_courses search=Bethpage) now returns 'Bethpage Red' as a mapped course. BONUS finding for the Red-3 par follow-up: the new Red row stores hole 3 as PAR 4 (the owner's '355y par 3' came from the pre-ingest course data). CAVEAT: the owner's EXISTING Red round only gains overlays after re-selecting the course (new rounds link automatically). Secrets Manager DATABASE_URL is STILL stale — refresh still owed.
items.why: Owner reported on TestFlight v1.1.5 (Bethpage Red hole 1, round in progress): no tee-shot yardage plates AND no hole centerline line. The overlays draw only from real CourseData.holes[i].features geometry with honest absence, so a bare hole means missing DB geometry. Fixing Red and generalising coverage audits protects the yardage-book feel on the owner's home course.

items.title: TOP-PRIORITY owner request: iOS edge-swipe-from-left to go back on ALL pages (except the in-round yardage-book hole page, which keeps prev-hole swipe)
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-12 (bundle #137 merged @c715d2e; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.5 build 202607121234). Previously: LANDED on integration/next (bundle PR #137) @9873d19 (cycle 111, 2026-07-12), NOTICEABLE (app-wide navigation). Mechanism (fable plan specs/universal-swipe-back-plan.md): a global JS left-screen-edge touch detector — WKWebView allowsBackForwardNavigationGestures rejected (static export output:'export' single-document pushState; native gesture renders stale snapshots for same-document history entries and is webview-global with no per-route exclusion). New frontend/src/components/nav/BackSwipe.tsx ('use client', renders null) mounted once in app/layout.tsx beside FloatingTabBar; document capture-phase passive touch listeners (never preventDefault). Pure core backSwipeGesture.ts: isEdgeStart (start x <= safeAreaLeft+24px), decideBackSwipe (rightward only, |dx|>1.8|dy|, flick <600ms&>=70px OR long-drag >=35%vw), isDisqualified (vertical-scroll early cancel), + guarded memoized readSafeAreaLeft() invalidated on orientationchange/resize. Route predicate shouldEnableBackSwipe.ts (sibling of shouldShowTabBar.ts): /round/new true, startsWith('/round/') false (covers /round/[id] AND /round/view), /map/course false, else true; re-checked at touchend for mid-gesture route races. Back semantics: router.back() gated by a session navigation-depth counter (window.history.length rejected as unusable in single-document export); depth 0 off-home -> router.push('/') so a deep-linked page never dead-ends or pops out of the app; depth 0 at '/' silent no-op; 350ms refire lockout. Conflict avoidance: edge-zone+direction+time gating; SwipeableRow (rightward swipe-to-delete, highest risk) gets an onPointerDownCapture edge-claim (stopPropagation on edge starts, the proven RoundPageClient trick) so a row never drags on an edge start; data-no-backswipe escape-hatch attribute on the three bottom sheets (CaddieSheet/LooperSheet/VoiceRoundSetupRealtime) + SwipeableRow confirm backdrop + FloatingTabBar root. 34 pure unit tests (backSwipeGesture 22 incl. pinned landscape boundary 83 true/84 false + shouldEnableBackSwipe 12). reviewer SHIP (all named failure modes — hijack in-content h-swipe / break hole nav / pop out of app / fire on non-edge / route race / listener leaks — traced and unfalsifiable; security surface nil, client-only touch math; tests genuine). qa PASS (gates on the integrated tree: lint / tsc / vitest 2379 / build 19-page static / voice 278; browser touch-event verification against the REAL listeners confirmed home<->tee-time history.back, deep-link push('/') fallback, home no-op, /round/ + /map/course exclusions, /round/new included, caddie-sheet data-no-backswipe blocks). designer APPROVE (threshold-only + haptic('light') — no follow-the-finger chrome — is the calm/native/yardage-book-correct call; no SaaS drift). Owner real-device pass needed (documented, not a gate fail): native map-pan non-hijack, native touch feel/haptic timing, and the SwipeableRow drag-vs-edge-flick distinction (framer needs real touch->pointer translation a headless browser can't produce).
items.why: Owner-requested directly: 'On every single page you should be able to swipe to go back... if I go to tee time from the home page, swiping back should take me there.' Standard iOS edge-back is an implicit expectation on every screen; its absence made client-side routes feel trapped. The in-round yardage-book page is excluded because its horizontal swipe already means prev/next hole.

items.title: TOP-PRIORITY owner bug: STT keyterm/priming context rendered as a user bubble in the LIVE caddie chat (and 'answered')
items.resolved: 6a68078 (cycle 105, integration/next / PR #136). Root cause: the golf keyterm/priming string (backend/app/caddie/keyterms.py build_transcription_prompt) is injected CORRECTLY as an STT hint at session.audio.input.transcription.prompt (never a conversation item, never in session.instructions) -- the leak is DOWNSTREAM: gpt-4o-transcribe hallucinates its own transcription.prompt back as the input transcript when server VAD false-triggers on silence/ambient noise (verbatim OR paraphrased -- the owner's two variants). frontend/src/lib/voice/realtime.ts then rendered ANY non-empty transcript as a role:'user' bubble; the model, hearing the same noise, independently replied 'Didn't catch that'. Fix (fable plan specs/caddie-context-leak-plan.md): new pure client classifier frontend/src/lib/voice/priming-echo.ts isPrimingEcho(transcript) wired as a drop-guard in the input_audio_transcription.completed handler (before onMessage AND before orderForUserTranscript -- ordering-safe, reservations are identity-keyed per realtime-ordering.ts), with a len-only realtime_priming_echo_dropped telemetry breadcrumb. Classifier drops iff A: signature phrase /player'?s clubs/ or /golf vocabulary/ (present in both owner variants, never in real speech), B: >=10 distinct GOLF_KEYTERMS (multi-word masked so 'double bogey' != 'bogey'; worst plausible real turn ~7), or C: >=3 segments ALL bare hazard nouns. Conservative: false-negatives render (safe), false-positives effectively impossible. Belt-and-suspenders backend rider: order-preserving hazard dedupe within the 'This hole:' sentence in keyterms.py. reviewer SHIP (no security/correctness blocker; /security-review clean -- regex from hardcoded GOLF_KEYTERMS only, escapeRegExp, no ReDoS, telemetry logs len not body, dedupe stays confined to transcription.prompt). qa PASS (7 gates on head a0c52d2/6a68078: lint / tsc / build / voice 278-278 / vitest 30-30 [priming-echo + realtime-warm] / ruff / backend test_transcription_prompt 10-10; classifier tests assert BOTH owner strings dropped AND real turns kept incl. adversarial dense-keyterm turn). designer APPROVE (pure subtraction -- no new chrome/placeholder where the bubble was; bubble styling untouched; legit user/typed turns unaffected).
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-11 (bundle #136 merged @71f8910; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.4 build 202607120035). Previously: LANDED on integration/next @6a68078 (bundle PR #136), NOTICEABLE.
items.why: Owner-reported directly with 2 screenshots (v1.1.3, on-course LIVE caddie): the priming/context string ('Player's clubs: GW, LW... This hole: trees, trees... Golf vocabulary: birdie, bogey...') showed as a dark right-aligned user bubble and the caddie tried to answer it ('Didn't catch that -- say again?'). Core-feature correctness: the priming must bias STT recognition but NEVER enter the visible/processed conversation.

items.title: Follow-up: lone 'Didn't catch that -- say again?' caddie reply fires on VAD false-triggers (noise), now with NO preceding user bubble
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-11 (bundle #136 merged @71f8910; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.4 build 202607120035). Previously: Landed @76d8c95 on integration/next (cycle 107, PR #136, NOTICEABLE caddie correctness): chose option (b) — client-side suppression of the lone no-input clarifier BUBBLE. New pure module noinput-clarifier.ts (isNoInputClarifier/couldBecomeClarifier, closed-vocabulary+marker+digit-ban, mirrors priming-echo.ts) + realtime.ts response<->input correlation + hold/suppress/release state machine (grace-timer bounded, no strand). NO VAD/threshold/mic/noise_reduction change (owner-gated line respected; option (a) untouched). Bias-to-keep: any real/failed/typed input turn never held. reviewer SHIP, qa PASS (lint/tsc clean, vitest 2288/2288, build ok, voice-smoke 278/278), designer APPROVE. Accepted residual: caddie still SPEAKS 'say again?' audio (only the bubble is suppressed; response.cancel deliberately avoided). Non-blocking follow-up filed: caddie-noise-clarifier-followups.
items.why: Flagged by BOTH reviewer and designer during caddie-context-leak review (cycle 105) as an explicitly-scoped-OUT residual of that fix (specs/caddie-context-leak-plan.md §6). The 'Didn't catch that' reply is the Realtime MODEL independently responding to committed noise-audio (input transcription is a side-channel; the model hears audio directly) -- a DIFFERENT system from the transcript-render leak that was fixed. Now that the fake priming user-bubble is correctly dropped, a VAD false-trigger on ambient noise can produce a lone caddie utterance with no user bubble above it -- reads as the caddie talking to itself (arguably MORE visually odd than before). Options (each its own plan): (a) tune server VAD so it doesn't fire on false-triggers -- the OPENAI_REALTIME_VAD=semantic_vad lever + server_vad threshold/noise_reduction knobs exist in realtime_relay.py but are owner-gated / explicitly hands-off in the context-leak fix; (b) suppress rendering (or generation) of a clarification reply that is a direct response to a turn we know was noise (e.g. no non-empty user transcript for that turn). User-facing caddie surface -> designer-gated. Do NOT touch VAD thresholds without an owner decision.

items.title: Follow-up nits from caddie-noise-clarification-reply review (cycle 107) — non-blocking
items.resolution: SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-12 (bundle #137 merged @c715d2e; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.5 build 202607121234). Previously: DONE on integration/next (cycle 109, @d187254, PR #137 bundle) as a focused realtime-voice reliability hardening pass. Plan on fable (specs/caddie-voice-reliability-hardening-plan.md). All 3 nits fixed + 2 audit fixes, all sandbox-verified (NO VAD/mic/gain/turn_detection/commit-timing change -- owner-gated line respected; reviewer grep-confirmed zero VAD surface): (1) attribution race -- replaced single-trigger triggerItemByResponse (LIFO pop) with candidate-SET triggerItemsByResponse + classifyCandidates() aggregate (ANY real=>release, ALL noinput=>suppress, else pending); a real utterance's clarifier can never be swallowed (strictly more conservative than shipped). (2) map lifecycle -- idempotent finishResponse() prunes per-turn + caps MAX_INPUT_CLASS_ENTRIES=64 / MAX_TRACKED=128 (evict-oldest, skip live candidates). (3) held-turn copy -- new pure lib/caddie/live-copy.ts liveEmptyStateHint 'speaking' branch so empty-state never says 'is listening' while footer says 'speaking' (designer APPROVE). (4a) response.done GA-shape id fallback (evt.response.id) -- no stuck-partial bubble. (4b) `if(!this.dc)return` post-cleanup guard -- late frames can't re-arm idle timer / leak to rebound handlers. New tests realtime-attribution (A1-A7) + realtime-lifecycle (L1-L3,4a,4b) + live-copy + ordering O1 + CaddieSheet.realtime, all proven RED pre-fix / GREEN post-fix; realtime-noinput.test.ts + priming-echo.test.ts unchanged (regression lock). reviewer SHIP, qa PASS (lint/tsc/vitest 2322/build/voice 278/ruff, backend diff empty), designer APPROVE. Non-blocking follow-up noted by designer: closed/error empty-state fallback still maps to the 'listening' hint (unreachable in live render today -- paused/classic-fallback intercepts; ticket only if that guarantee changes).
items.why: Three non-blocking nits raised by reviewer + designer on the SHIPPED no-input clarifier suppression (@76d8c95), tracked so they aren't lost. (1) Narrow item-attribution race in realtime.ts: pendingSpeechItems.pop() (LIFO+clear) at response.created can misattribute a REAL-but-garbled turn's clarifier to a phantom noise speech_started that fired inside the sub-second turn->response.created window, hiding a LEGIT clarifier bubble (audio still plays; no substantive answer ever lost -- degrades to the accepted audio-only residual). Fix idea: on a 'real' transcript, re-scan triggerItemByResponse/heldResponses and un-suppress. (2) triggerItemByResponse + inputClassByItem maps are pruned only at cleanup() -> one entry/turn growth within a session (bounded by 90s idle-disconnect; not a real leak). Fix idea: delete the pair's entries when a response finalizes/resolves. (3) Cosmetic: during a held/suppressed turn CaddieSheet.tsx empty-state copy ('...is listening') can briefly (<=2s grace) disagree with the footer status ('speaking'). Fix idea: branch the empty-state copy on 'speaking' too. All low-severity; batch into a future voice-caddie pass, not worth its own cycle.

items.title: Guide validator: _has_side_flip binds a hazard carry to the nearest same-type keyword regardless of side -> both-sides multi-bunker sentence false-reject (Bethpage Black 11)
items.why: OBSERVED cycle 116 (2026-07-12) on Bethpage BLACK 11 regen: a CORRECTLY-grounded guide 'thread the gap between the 245-left bunker and the 270/325 right-side bunkers' is REJECTED because _has_side_flip binds every plausible in-window number to the nearest same-type hazard keyword's SIDE -- the right-side 325 falls inside the LEFT bunker keyword's _SIDE_WINDOW_WORDS window, is checked against the LEFT cluster {245,415} runs, and fails (325 not grounded left) -> whole guide rejects. Every number is actually grounded to its TRUE side; the binding window just cannot separate two adjacent CROSS-SIDE bunker references in one sentence. This binding is the intentional anti-laundering / fail-closed-on-ambiguity control (5e4b861 carry-tie-break laundering fix), so a change REQUIRES full adversarial treatment: e.g. check a bound number against the union of the sides its two nearest same-type keywords name, OR bind a number only to its single nearest same-type keyword, WITHOUT re-opening the co-located-false-number laundering the current all-in-window binding closes. Fable plan + eval-harness teeth (each new accept-case RED pre-fix) + adversarial review. FILES: backend/app/caddie/guide_writer.py (_has_side_flip binding, _side_and_carry_supported). Honest-empty is SAFE until fixed (caddie falls back to the grounded generic hazard line). FORBIDDEN to inline-fix per tasks/lessons.md.
items.resolution: CYCLE 118 (2026-07-13): FIX LANDED on integration/next (commit 12cec56; specs/guide-validator-cross-side-binding-plan.md, fable plan). _has_side_flip now binds each in-window carry number to the side word nearest THAT number (new helper _attributed_side; a tie over side-VALUES collapses to the keyword nearest_side = the cycle-115 fail-closed binding), and the keyword nearest_side side-only check runs UNCONDITIONALLY (closes a reattribution escape). items.title: Guide validator: a carry number of one hazard TYPE falls inside another type's keyword window and is validated against the WRONG type's geometry -> false-reject (Bethpage Black 7/11 residual)
items.why: OBSERVED cycle 118 (2026-07-13) on-box, AFTER the cross-SIDE binding fix (12cec56) landed and was proven: a fresh Black-11 candidate still rejected -- 'trees at 5 and 190 yards right and the bunkers at 270 and 325 right' binds the TREES carry 190 (real geometry: trees right 190) into the nearby 'bunkers' keyword's _SIDE_WINDOW_WORDS window; _has_side_flip attributes 190 to right and checks it against BUNKER geometry (right bunkers {270,325,420} runs), where 190 is not grounded -> whole guide rejects though 190 is a correctly-grounded right TREES carry. Root: _has_side_flip binds numbers to same-window HAZARD KEYWORDS and now to per-number SIDE, but never verifies the number's nearest keyword is the SAME hazard TYPE it is being validated against -- cross-type adjacency (a trees number next to a bunker word, or vice versa) poisons the check. This (plus LLM verbosity: play_line 397>240 on Black 7) is the residual keeping Black 7/11 honest-empty (Black 16/18); the cross-side defect itself is fixed. This was pre-noted in guide-validator-carry-span/cross-side plans as the known cross-TYPE pollution limitation (explicitly out of the cross-side fix's scope). Fix direction: bind each number to its nearest hazard occurrence and validate ONLY against that occurrence's TYPE; or drop numbers whose nearest hazard keyword is a different type than the one under check. Same fail-closed bar -- must not let a genuinely wrong-side/wrong-type number launder through. Fable plan + adversarial review. FILES: backend/app/caddie/guide_writer.py (_has_side_flip number binding). Honest-empty is SAFE. FORBIDDEN to inline-fix per tasks/lessons.md.
items.resolution: CYCLE 119 (2026-07-13): FIXED + proven on integration/next (code commit d4c3db5, PR #140; specs/guide-validator-cross-type-number-binding-plan.md, FABLE plan). _has_side_flip now attributes each in-window carry number to the hazard-keyword occurrence grammatically NEAREST to that number across ALL present types, and checks it ONLY against that occurrence's type (new _owns_number: a strictly-nearer DIFFERENT-type occurrence steals the number; cross-type distance TIE is NOT a steal -> checked vs every tied type = fail-closed, reproduces old on ties). KEY fable correction: trees has NO keyword in _HAZARD_KEYWORD_TO_TYPE, so the literal 'nearest keyword across present types' rule could not even fix the incident -> added an OWNERSHIP-ONLY _TREES_BINDING_PATTERN (_NUMBER_BINDING_PATTERNS), used solely inside _has_side_flip for number ownership + re-routed-number checking, DELIBERATELY not in the type scan (adding it would newly false-reject honest tree mentions on OSM-sparse holes). Plan 2a strict-superset proof: single-present-type fields (all cross-side r1-r9/p1-p12, carry-span, cycle-115 tests) byte-identical; the only verdict flips are numbers strictly nearer a different present-type keyword (the grammatically-cross-type-correct set). reviewer(fresh opus) SHIP -- type scan byte-identical, tests pure-add/unweakened, occurrence-hoisting a pure relocation, no bypass reproducible (window-boundary <=6/<7 + strict-< tie, fabricated-stolen-by-trees rejects at owner, side-flip still caught by unconditional nearest_side check). qa PASS -- ruff clean; 175/175 validator suite (160 baseline + 15 new: P1-P4 pass, R1-R7 reject, _owns_number units); pure-add test diff. CI PR #140 all gates SUCCESS. ON-BOX REGEN (sanctioned, bounded cap=2, key-free SSM, d4c3db5 worktree, deploy repo untouched on main@2f7f486, worktree removed after): Black 16/18 -> 17/18.
